patch.value: ADR: adopt "repository-native project context engine" as the product category; pm as master brand over CLI/MCP/SDK/Web/Packages surfaces (proposed)

Human-gated positioning decision. Ratifies the three-level category system (agent-native project management as searchable category, repository-native project context engine as differentiated category, context infrastructure as vision), the pm master brand with named surfaces, the doctrine line, and the exact public metadata copy — before any public copy changes ship.

Maintainer explicitly accepts or amends the category statement, brand architecture, doctrine, and metadata copy; rejected alternative categories recorded; acceptance unblocks the Phase-1 message-surface rewrite

# ADR: product category and brand architecture (proposed)

## Context
Current public descriptor "Git-native project management CLI for humans and agents" compresses the product into a crowded category (Markdown task trackers, terminal Kanban) and hides the strongest capabilities: explainable context ranking, durable plans, evidence graph, replayable history, typed SDK, MCP/skills surfaces, context-quality evaluation. Repo shows 1300+ commits and dozens of releases against single-digit stars — a positioning/distribution/proof gap, not a feature gap. The master brand is currently perceived as "pm-cli" (an interface), while the product spans CLI, SDK, MCP, plugins, Web, packages, search adapters, profiles, and an evaluation framework.

## Decision (proposed)
1. Category statement: **"pm is the repository-native project context engine for coding agents and software teams."** Supporting: "It turns work into compact, queryable, auditable context: ranked next actions, durable plans, dependencies, decisions, evidence, and history — stored in Git and available through CLI, MCP, TypeScript SDK, and Web."
2. Three-level category system: familiar/searchable = "agent-native project management" (SEO + comparison pages only); differentiated = "repository-native project context engine"; vision = "context infrastructure for software work".
3. Doctrine: "Project management is context management." Promise: "Give every coding agent the right project context." Campaign: "Your coding agent can write code. pm tells it what matters."
4. Master brand = **pm**; surfaces named pm CLI / pm MCP / pm SDK / pm Web / pm Packages. Technical identifiers (unbraind/pm-cli, @unbrained/pm-cli, pm, pm-mcp, /sdk subpaths) unchanged — no rename.
5. Exact metadata copy (GitHub About, npm description, website title/meta, social card, topics/keywords) as recorded in note (5) on parent pm-wjfa.

## Rejected alternatives (do not relitigate)
- "AI project manager" — implies autonomous personality, not dependable infrastructure.
- "Agent memory" — Beads occupies it; understates workflow, evidence, governance.
- "Task tracker for AI" — undersells the system.
- "MCP project manager" — ties identity to one transport.
- "Context OS" — too grand before the category exists.
- "AI-powered project management" — generic; falsely implies model dependence.
- "CLI-first Jira replacement" — frames around incumbents instead of the agent problem.
- Renaming the package/binary — destroys compatibility and search equity for no positioning gain.

## Consequences
- Phase-1 message-surface rewrite (README/website/About/npm/topics) is blocked on this acceptance; drift-reconciliation and agent-distribution work is NOT (correctness ships regardless).
- "Why context engine": the system stores, selects/ranks, delivers, updates, and evaluates context, and preserves continuity — "project" keeps it concrete vs generic RAG; "repository-native" carries local-first, reviewable, portable, no mandatory backend.
- All public counts (commands/tools/capabilities/packages) become generated from contracts/catalog, never hand-written, so the category claim is never undermined by drift.
